title: Price transparency
body: "This guidance explains how businesses and traders must be transparent when displaying pricing information.  It covers:   what to include in your pricing information (including mandatory fees, taxes and charges) ‘drip pricing’ (when prices are added as consumers proceed with a transaction) ‘partitioned pricing’ (when component parts of a price are given, but the overall price a customer would pay is not)   We have also published a short summary of the guidance, with visual examples to show how the law may apply in practice.  Find out more about treating customers fairly when selling online, including:   getting consent for additional charges other unfair commercial practices  "
